sections.name: Front Matter and Overview
topics: cover page
copyright and license
contact information
table of contents
product overview
sections.notes: Pages 1-2 are cover; page 3 is copyright/contact; pages 4-5 are table of contents; page 6 is Overview. No configuration content.

sections.name: About LCC — Concepts and Definitions
topics: LCC network overview
Node definition
Segment definition
Line definition
Consumer events
Producer events
sections.notes: Defines Consumer/Producer event model (section 1.1.4–1.1.5) and Line state/veto concepts. Key background for event role classification in profile-1.

sections.name: Hardware — Features, Specs, and Physical Connections
topics: Tower-LCC electrical specifications
CAN LCC RJ-45 connector pinout
Power connections (7.5–27 VDC)
Status indicators (green ON, red ACT)
Blue/Gold buttons and LEDs
I/O connector wiring (16 lines across 2 ports)
Getting started with CDI tools (JMRI, DecoderPro)
sections.notes: Section 2 (features/specs) on pages 9-10; Section 3 (connections) on pages 10-13; Section 4 (getting started / CDI intro) on pages 13-14. Relevant to recipes for initial setup.

sections.name: Input/Output Configuration
topics: Node Identification (Name, Description)
Output Function options (No Function, Steady, Pulse, Blink, Sample variants)
Input Function options (Disabled, Active Hi/Lo, Alt Action, Sample)
Delay timers (Interval 1 on-delay, Interval 2 off-delay, Retrigger)
Commands — consumer events and actions (On, Off, Change, Veto, Gated)
Indications — producer events and triggers (Output State, Function, Input, Gated)
sections.notes: Core CDI segment 'Port I/O' (space 253, origin 128) with 16 replicated Line groups. Consumer events are section 5.3.4 Commands (p.19); Producer events are section 5.3.5 Indications (p.20). Relevance rules exist: input functions are irrelevant when Output Function is not 'No Function' (and vice versa); Delay fields are conditionally used based on output type.

sections.name: Tower LCC Logic — Conditionals, Variables, and Producers
topics: Logic conditional structure (Group, Last/Single, Ladder Group)
Mast conditional evaluation order (most to least restrictive)
Action/exit format table (Send then Exit, Send then Evaluate Next, Exit, Evaluate Next)
Action conditions (None, Immediately, After Delay, Immediate/Delayed if True/False)
Variables (V1, V2) — true/false event pairs, state retention
Logic functions (AND, OR, XOR, AND/OR change, AND Then, V1 Only, V2 Only, null)
Delay timer (0–60,000 ms/s/min, Retrigger option)
Producers — up to 4 output events per conditional (None, Immediately, After Delay)
sections.notes: CDI segment for Logic Conditionals (32 conditionals). Variable events are consumers; conditional output events are producers. Relevance rules: many fields become irrelevant when Group Type is 'Blocked (0)'; Action fields depend on exit format selection. Key for profile-2.

sections.name: Track Circuits — Virtual Code Lines
topics: Prototype coded track circuit background
Simulating code lines with LCC events
Linking virtual code lines via CDI (TX key from transmitting node)
Block numbering (1–8 per node, up to 8 virtual code lines)
Prototype code packet structure (Start, Speed/Indication 2/3/4/6/7/8/9, code 5, M)
ABS and APB signal examples
sections.notes: CDI segment for Track/Virtual Code Lines. Each track circuit has TX and RX event groups — TX events are producers, RX events are consumers. Profile-5 recipes for linking virtual code lines at modular meets.

sections.name: Compatible Input/Output Cards
topics: BOD-4 DCC block occupancy detector (4 block + 4 I/O)
BOD4-CP DCC BOD (4 block, 4 inputs, 2 turnout drivers)
BOD-8 DCC block occupancy detector (8 block, CT coils)
OIB-8 opto-isolator board (8 input)
SCSD-8 single coil solenoid driver
SMD-8 stall motor driver (8 line, H-bridge)
RB-4 relay board (4x SPDT)
FOB-A fan-out board (ribbon to compression terminals)
BOB-S break-out board (ribbon to screw terminals)
sections.notes: No direct CDI fields here, but relevant for recipes: describes wiring conventions (e.g., RB-4 active-low inputs require 'Active Lo' output config, BOD-8 outputs are low during detection). Useful background for profile-5 configuration recipes.

sections.name: Troubleshooting, Boot Loader, and Maintenance
topics: Sanity test procedure (power LED, alias establishment)
Activity test using JMRI LCC Monitor
Boot loader upgrade (nodes #02.01.57.00.00.76 or earlier)
Firmware upgrade procedure (JMRI Firmware Update, .hex file selection)
Grounding and isolation considerations
Warranty information (1 year, no user serviceable parts)
FCC compliance information
sections.notes: Section 9 troubleshooting (p.29-30), Section 10 boot loader (p.30-31), Section 11 grounding (p.32), Section 12 warranty (p.32), Section 13 FCC (p.32-33). Only firmware upgrade steps are recipe-relevant for profile-5.

appendices.name: Electrical Specifications
appendices.purpose: I/O port pin-level current ratings for Port 1 (pins 1,3,7,9 limited to 2mA; others 25mA) and Port 2 (all 25mA); 200mA combined maximum across all lines.

appendices.name: CAN LCC RJ-45 Pinout
appendices.purpose: Pin-by-pin wiring table for the LCC CAN RJ-45 connector (CAN H, CAN L, GND, Alt L/H DCC, GND, +Power).

appendices.name: I/O Connector Wiring Table
appendices.purpose: Maps 10-pin IDC connector pin numbers to Tower LCC line numbers (lines run opposite to pin numbers).

appendices.name: Logic Function Reference Table
appendices.purpose: Complete list of logic operators: V1 AND V2, V1 OR V2, V1 XOR V2, change variants, V1 AND Then V2, V1 Only, V2 Only, null.

appendices.name: Prototype Code Line Packet Structure
appendices.purpose: Describes prototype Genrakode/Electrocode packet: Start bit, Speed/Indication codes (2,3,4,6,7,8,9), code 5, and M code; explains how Tower LCC maps these to 16 EventID pairs.
